topic.name: Sustainable Energy Solutions
topic.description: The documents discuss various strategies and technologies aimed at promoting sustainable energy practices and reducing environmental impact.

doc2: case studies on solar power implementation

topic.name: sustainable energy solutions
topic.description: The documents discuss various approaches and technologies aimed at promoting sustainable energy practices.

doc1: impact of solar power on reducing carbon emissions
doc3: role of biofuels in sustainable development
challenges in transitioning to green energy

topic.name: Sustainable Energy Solutions
topic.description: The documents discuss various approaches and technologies for promoting sustainable energy practices.

doc2: impact of wind energy on local economies
doc3: role of government policies in energy transition

topic.name: Medicare Policy and Access
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving Medicare coverage, payment structures, and access to healthcare services for seniors and specific patient populations.

Document 1: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act (SSA) to direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services to collect data on the differences, if any, between payments to physicians for immune globulins.
Requires the Secretary also to review data on the access of eligible individuals to immune globulins.
Document 2: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act (SSA) to keep the same Medicare part B premium for 2010 that was in effect for 2009.

Directs the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) of the Department of Labor to study and report to Congress on whether a consumer price index for elderly consumers (CPI-E) more accurately reflects the true costs of inflation for elderly Americans.
Document 3: Amends titles XVIII (Medicare) and XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to include qualified clinical psychologists, clinical social workers, psychiatric hospitals, mental health treatment facilities, and substance abuse treatment facilities within the health professionals eligible for incentive payments.
Requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to award grants to eligible entities to facilitate the purchase of health information technology.

topic.name: Tax Legislation for Individuals
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at modifying tax laws to benefit individuals, particularly seniors and commuters.

Document 1: Complete and Permanent Property Tax Deduction Act of 2009
make permanent the standard tax deduction for real property taxes
Document 2: Seniors' Tax Simplification Act of 2013
new federal income tax Form 1040SR for individuals who have turned 65
Document 3: Commuter Parity Act of 2015
modify the exclusion from gross income for transportation benefits provided by an employer

topic.name: Trade Policy and Tariffs
topic.description: This topic covers legislative amendments related to trade policies, tariffs, and duties on various goods in the context of U.S. trade law.

Document 1: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to apply countervailing duties to nonmarket economies.
Prohibits the administering authority from considering requests for market economy treatment at the individual business enterprise level.
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to suspend temporarily the duty on electric pneumatic airsoft rifles.
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on AC electric motors.

topic.name: Military Records and Intelligence Oversight
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the management, disclosure, and oversight of military records and intelligence activities in the United States.

Document 1: requires the National Archives and Records Administration (NARA) to establish the Missing Armed Forces Personnel Records Collection
establishes the Missing Armed Forces Personnel Records Review Board
Document 2: Authorizes appropriations for FY2009 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities
specifies that the amounts authorized and the authorized personnel ceilings are those specified in the classified Schedule of Authorizations
Document 3: directs the Secretary of Defense to develop and promulgate guidelines for the Department of Defense
requires the Secretary to report annually to Congress on Army arsenal capabilities, workload, performance, and required capital investments

topic.name: Public Land Management and Protection
topic.description: Legislation focused on the management, protection, and recreational use of federal public lands.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to transfer to the Secretary of the Navy administrative jurisdiction over certain public lands
Requires the Secretary of the Navy to prepare an integrated natural resources management plan with respect to the transferred lands.
Document 2: Requires federal public land management officials to facilitate the use of, and access to, federal public lands for fishing, sport hunting, and recreational shooting
Requires Bureau of Land Management (BLM) and Forest Service lands to be open to recreational fishing, hunting, and shooting unless the managing agency acts to close such lands.
Document 3: Designates specified lands in the Grand Mesa, Uncompahgre, and Gunnison National Forests as wilderness areas
Withdraws lands designated as wilderness or a special management area from all forms of entry, appropriation, or disposal under public land laws.

topic.name: Veterans' Services Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing services and support for veterans, including healthcare access, employment training, and electronic resource management.

Document 1: amends the Veterans Access, Choice, and Accountability Act of 2014
expand eligibility for the Veterans Choice Program
Document 2: Vocational Employment and Technical Skills Act
require a state applicant for a grant or contract for certain veterans' employment and training programs
Document 3: Improving Veterans' Electronic Transition Services Act
develop and implement a system allowing a veteran to securely and electronically access his or her military personnel file

topic.name: Immigration Reform Legislation
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at amending the Immigration and Nationality Act to address specific visa categories and immigration enforcement issues.

Document 1: H-2A Improvement Act - Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to include within the H-2A nonimmigrant visa category (temporary agricultural workers)
Provides for a three-year initial period of admission with additional three-year extensions.
Document 2: ARTS Require Timely Service (ARTS) Act - Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to require the Secretary of Homeland Security to adjudicate a visa petition
States that if such petition is not adjudicated within the required time frame...
Document 3: Safe Roads Enhancement Act of 2008 - Amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to include within the definition of 'aggravated felony'
Makes inadmissible or deportable an alien who has been convicted of driving while intoxicated.

topic.name: Regulatory Standards for Consumer Products
topic.description: Legislation focused on establishing safety and labeling requirements for various consumer products including drugs, food, and cosmetics.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require a liquid formulation of an over-the-counter drug to be packaged with a dosage delivery device meeting specified requirements.
Deems a drug to be misbranded if it does not meet the requirements of this Act.
Document 2: Directs the Commissioner of Food and Drugs (FDA) to promulgate a final regulation revising federal nutrition labeling regulations to require the label of a food that has less than .5 grams of trans fat per serving to express that information with an asterisk or other notation.
expressing the trans fat content as zero when the trans fat content of such food is declared in the nutrition information on the label.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to require the registration of cosmetic products and cosmetic manufacturing facilities.
Deems a cosmetic product adulterated if its manufacture, packing, or holding do not conform to current good manufacturing practice regulations.

topic.name: Aviation Safety and Technology
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving safety, tracking, and communication technologies in aviation to enhance the response to emergencies and missing aircraft incidents.

Document 1: study the feasibility of technology to improve communications between commercial aircraft and air traffic control
pinpoint the location of missing aircraft within 30 minutes
Document 2: issue regulations to ensure that a covered aircraft is equipped with technology that provides for the continuous tracking of information
timely and cost effective recovery of the cockpit voice recorder and flight data recorder
Document 3: Repeals the prohibition against an air carrier's operation of an aircraft nonstop in air transportation
Prohibits the obligation of federal funds to enforce the Port Authority of New York and New Jersey perimeter rule

topic.name: Unemployment Compensation Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at extending and modifying unemployment compensation programs and addressing wage discrimination in employment.

Document 1: Rescinds $9.2 billion in discretionary, unexpired funds out of all available unobligated federal funds.
Extends the final date for entering a federal-state agreement under the Emergency Unemployment Compensation (EUC) program through May 5, 2010.
Document 2: Amends the Civil Rights Act of 1964 to declare that an unlawful employment practice occurs when a discriminatory compensation decision or other practice is adopted.
Allows liability to accrue, and allows an aggrieved person to obtain relief, including recovery of back pay, for up to two years preceding the filing of the charge.
Document 3: Amends the Supplemental Appropriations Act, 2008 to extend emergency unemployment compensation (EUC) payments for eligible individuals to weeks of employment ending on or before April 1, 2014.
Amends the Assistance for Unemployed Workers and Struggling Families Act to extend until March 31, 2014, requirements that federal payments to states cover 100% of EUC.

topic.name: Government Property and Services Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses the management and conveyance of government-owned properties and the regulation of essential public services such as postal and aviation operations.

Document 1: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to convey and quitclaim all interest of the United States in and to a parcel of real property
Requires the Conservation District to pay to the Secretary the appraised market value of the land
Document 2: Prohibits the United States Postal Service (USPS) from closing any post office that does not have another post office within eight miles
Protecting Our Rural Post Offices Act of 2011
Document 3: reauthorizes for the period March 31, 2016, through July 15, 2016, the airport improvement program
Department of Transportation authority to appropriate funds to acquire, establish, and improve air navigation facilities

topic.name: Disaster Response and Business Support
topic.description: Legislation aimed at supporting small businesses affected by disasters and regulating greenhouse gas emissions.

Document 1: Amends the Small Business Act to provide that if a small business is affected by a catastrophic incident...
Deems the 2005 Hurricanes Katrina, Wilma, and Rita as catastrophic incidents for purposes of this Act.
Document 2: Establishment of a federal greenhouse gas registry that will collect reliable and accurate data...
Defines 'greenhouse gas' as carbon dioxide, methane, nitrous oxide, hydrofluorocarbons...
Document 3: Directs the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to amend the 2006 hurricane season disaster response plan...
Report monthly to the congressional small business committees on the operation of the SBA disaster loan program.

topic.name: U.S. Postal Service Legislation
topic.description: Legislation governing the operations, accountability, and financial management of the United States Postal Service (USPS).

Document 1: Amends provisions of federal law relating to the United States Postal Service (USPS) with respect to its reorganization, financial management, and workforce.
Requires USPS to develop and submit to the Commission a plan for the closure or consolidation of postal retail facilities.
Document 2: Directs the U.S. Postal Service (USPS), in making a determination whether to close or consolidate a post office, to consider the economic effects on the community served by such post office.
Establishes a waiting period and appeal procedures for USPS determinations to close or consolidate mail processing facilities.
Document 3: Directs the Postmaster General to provide for the issuance of a forever stamp that honors the sacrifices of the members of the U.S. Armed Forces who have been prisoners, missing, or unaccounted for.

topic.name: Election and Governance Reforms
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at ensuring fair electoral processes and governance structures for various groups in the United States.

Document 1: Prohibits a state that has been redistricted after an apportionment from being redistricted again until after the next apportionment of Representatives
Requires such redistricting to be conducted through a plan developed by the independent redistricting commission established in the state
Document 2: Recognizes the right of the Native Hawaiian people to reorganize a Native Hawaiian governing entity
Requires those members, in consultation with the Secretary of the Interior, to establish an Interim Governing Council

Document 3: Amends the National Labor Relations Act to make it an unfair labor practice for an employer to recognize or bargain collectively with a labor organization that has not been selected by a majority of the employees in a secret ballot election

topic.name: Child Care and Reproductive Health Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to child care funding and reproductive health policies, including abortion funding restrictions.

Document 1: Amends the Child Care and Development Block Grant Act of 1990 to authorize appropriations to carry out the Child Care and Development Block Grant program
Increases from 4% to 15% the maximum amount of child care and development block grant funds that a state may use for specified activities designed to improve the quality of child care.
Document 2: Amends the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act to require the Director of the Office of Personnel Management to ensure that no multi-state qualified health plan offers coverage of abortion
Prohibits any state law from preempting the coverage limitations of this Act.
Document 3: Prohibits a federal official from expending federal funds for any foreign or domestic population control or population planning program
Prohibits federal funds for family planning activity including abortion procedures.

topic.name: Educational Equity and Support Programs
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and initiatives aimed at enhancing educational resources and support for diverse student populations, particularly focusing on Indian students and equitable funding for educational institutions.

Document 1: establish a program through which the Bureau of Indian Affairs (BIA) shall contract with eligible entities for the purpose of providing educational benefits to Indian students
establish targeted, culturally sensitive, dropout prevention activities
Document 3: establishes the Citizens' Commission on Educational Equity
ensuring that schools receive adequate resources

topic.name: Legislative Amendments and Land Use
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative amendments related to environmental management, land conveyance, and education policy.

Document 1: Amends the Oil Pollution Act of 1990
exempt advances to the Coast Guard
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to convey to the town of Mantua
use the land conveyed under this Act for public purposes
Document 3: Modifies the calculation of basic, concentration, targeted, and education finance incentive grants
increase the minimum number of days in their school year

topic.name: Legislative Measures on Price and Insurance Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative acts aimed at regulating pricing practices during crises and improving transparency in insurance policies.

Document 1: Makes it unlawful for any person, during a proclaimed international crisis affecting the oil market, to sell gasoline...
Requires the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) to enforce a violation of this Act as an unfair or deceptive act or practice.
Document 2: This bill amends the Federal Crop Insurance Act to eliminate a requirement that any renegotiated Standard Insurance Agreement (SRA) be budget neutral.
Eliminating the budget neutrality requirement permits USDA to use the renegotiation of the SRA to achieve savings.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to request the National Association of Insurance Commissioners (NAIC) to review and describe...
Requires each state to require any issuer of a long-term care insurance policy to use such form when marketing such a policy in the state.

topic.name: Federal Legislative Reforms
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative acts aimed at improving access to justice, transparency in government, and the rights of individuals in civil actions.

Document 1: Amends the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act to include expert witness fees within the definition of 'attorneys' fees'.
may be awarded to the prevailing party in a civil action.
Document 2: Amends the federal judicial code with respect to the jurisdiction of federal courts over civil actions.
Requires the court presiding over the action that was filed later to stay the later action until the action that was filed first is no longer pending.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Advisory Committee Act to require appointments to advisory committees to be made without regard to political affiliation.
Requires agency heads to make available on the agency's Internet site specified information about the advisory committee and its activities.

topic.name: Housing and Water Resource Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts related to housing assistance and water resource management in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Water Resources Development Act of 1999 to direct the Secretary of the Army to enter into a local cooperation agreement for a municipal water supply system.
Requires the agreement to provide for the development by local officials of a water supply project.
Document 2: Directs the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System and other agencies to study capital requirements for mortgage servicing assets for banking institutions.
Document 3: Authorizes the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development to provide financial assistance to develop community-based housing organizations.
Prohibits the use of funds authorized by this Act to lobby or retain a lobbyist.

topic.name: Legislation for Vulnerable Populations
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative measures aimed at protecting and supporting vulnerable groups, including missing seniors and individuals facing housing discrimination.

Document 1: establish a national Silver Alert communications network
award grants to states for support of Silver Alert plans
Document 2: make a technical amendment with respect to the timing of payments
specify that a creditor may not treat a payment on a credit card account as late
Document 3: prohibited discrimination in housing sales and rentals
adds to the list of prohibited actual or perceived bases sexual orientation, gender identity

topic.name: Legislative Amendments and Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at amending existing laws to enhance regulatory frameworks and address specific issues related to oil spill liability, tribal fund distribution, and customs enforcement.

Document 1: Amends the Oil Pollution Act of 1990 to increase the liability of the party responsible for an offshore facility
direct the President to promulgate regulations to allow advance payments from the Oil Spill Liability Trust Fund
Document 2: Amends the Indian Tribal Judgment Funds Use or Distribution Act to exclude funds paid by Minnesota
from federal or state income taxes
Document 3: Amends the Tariff Act of 1930 to require the Commissioner responsible for U.S. Customs and Border Protection to investigate claims of evasion
Defines 'evasion' to mean entering covered merchandise into U.S. customs territory by means of any document or electronically transmitted data
